title: centaurea resupinata subsp. lagascae
intro: Centaurea resupinata subsp. lagascae, also known as Lagasca's knapweed or Lagasca's star-thistle, is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea resupinata subsp. lagascae include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other medicinal products.
